ddls:
CREATE TABLE "weather_stations" (
  "location_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each weather station. */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather station. */,
  "country_id" TEXT /* Identifier for the country where the station is located. */,
  "country" TEXT /* Name of the country. */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the weather station. */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the weather station. */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the weather station in meters. */,
  "time_zone" TEXT /* Time zone of the weather station. */,
  "station_type" TEXT /* Type of weather station (e.g., urban, rural, coastal). */,
  "operational_status" TEXT /* Operational status of the weather station (e.g., active, inactive, maintenance). */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "temperature_readings" (
  "reading_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each temperature reading. */,
  "location_id" TEXT /* Reference to the weather station's location ID. */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year when the temperature reading was recorded. */,
  "month" INTEGER /* Month when the temperature reading was recorded. */,
  "day" INTEGER /* Day when the temperature reading was recorded. */,
  "hour" INTEGER /* Hour when the temperature reading was recorded. */,
  "reading_timestamp" TEXT /* Precise timestamp for the temperature reading. */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature recorded in degrees Celsius. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reading_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_temperature_readings_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each country. */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country. */,
  "region" TEXT /* Geographic region of the country. */,
  "population" TEXT /* Population of the country. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user. */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user. */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user. */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, analyst, admin). */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication. */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login. */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department or team the user belongs to. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event. */,
  "reading_id" INTEGER /* ID of the temperature reading being accessed. */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the reading. */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the reading was accessed. */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download). */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the data. */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used for access. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_reading_id FOREIGN KEY ("reading_id") REFERENCES temperature_readings ("reading_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "temperature_statistics" (
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each temperature statistic. */,
  "location_id" TEXT /* Reference to the weather station's location ID. */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year for which the statistics are calculated. */,
  "month" INTEGER /* Month for which the statistics are calculated. */,
  "average_temp" REAL /* Average temperature for the time frame. */,
  "max_temp" REAL /* Maximum temperature recorded for the time frame. */,
  "min_temp" REAL /* Minimum temperature recorded for the time frame. */,
  "std_deviation" REAL /* Standard deviation of temperatures for the time frame. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_temperature_statistics_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert. */,
  "location_id" TEXT /* Reference to the weather station's location ID. */,
  "threshold_temp" REAL /* Temperature threshold that triggered the alert. */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., high temperature, low temperature). */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was triggered. */,
  "severity_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high). */,
  "resolved_status" INTEGER /* Status indicating if the alert has been resolved. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report. */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report. */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was generated. */,
  "report_summary" TEXT /* Summary of the report. */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., monthly, annual, special). */,
  "report_file_path" TEXT /* File path or URL of the generated report. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "temperature_forecasts" (
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each temperature forecast. */,
  "location_id" TEXT /* Reference to the weather station's location ID. */,
  "forecast_date" TEXT /* Date for which the temperature is predicted. */,
  "predicted_temp" REAL /* Predicted temperature in degrees Celsius. */,
  "forecast_model" TEXT /* Model used for the temperature forecast. */,
  "confidence_interval" REAL /* Confidence interval of the forecast. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_temperature_forecasts_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("location_id")
)
